demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents, who are his primary carers

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: His legs are locked completely stiff, and he is crying in agonizing pain every time we try to move him. (Reported by his mother — he has cerebral palsy and this came on suddenly a few hours ago.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It started about four hours ago and hasn't let up — his usual leg-relaxing medicine isn't touching it and he's in too much pain for us to move him, feed him, or clean him.

symptom_narrative.onset: about four hours ago, quite suddenly
symptom_narrative.character: his affected leg has gone completely rigid and locked out straight; any attempt to move or bend it makes him scream, and his whole body tenses and startles
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst spasms we've ever seen — he is crying constantly and inconsolable
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: mainly his right leg, from the hip down; the knee is locked straight and the foot is pointing down
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, with painful jolts whenever he is touched or moved
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — his regular liquid baclofen given two hours ago made no difference
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he is drooling and his speech is slurred, but that is how he usually is; right now he can't be fed because of the distress
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has muscle stiffness and spasms as part of his cerebral palsy, but never this severe or this locked
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally he walks with aids using a stiff, crossing, tip-toe gait; right now he is completely rigid and stuck in bed

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: cerebral palsy — he was diagnosed as a baby, had delayed milestones and has weakness down one side
past_medical_history.how_managed: regular liquid baclofen, physiotherapy, and mobility aids

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing tight, stiff muscles and spasms in his legs (baseline)
he normally drools and has slurred speech
past_medical_history.how_managed: baseline for him, no change today

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: cared for full-time at home by both parents; today they cannot reposition him or manage his hygiene because any movement causes severe pain. His wheelchair is parked out of reach and his bed does not have a pressure-relieving mattress.

communication_style.typical_message_style: parents relaying on behalf of a non-verbal / minimally-verbal child in acute distress

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: known cerebral palsy with delayed motor milestones and spastic hemiplegia; baseline dysarthria and drooling; communicates in limited words and through distress cues rather than describing pain in detail

objective_data_offered.value: right leg held rigidly straight, knee locked in extension and foot pointing downward; he cannot bend it and screams when it is touched
he looks flushed and is crying loudly and continuously, clearly in severe distress
the skin over his knee and ankle looks intact — no obvious wound, redness or open sore that we can see
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer visual impression only, not a clinical examination

objective_data_offered.value: he had a normal bowel motion this morning and his tummy feels soft, not swollen, when we rest a hand on it
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer report, not a clinical abdominal examination
